question: Who was the first player to hit a 9-dart finish on live television?
explanation: John Lowe achieved the first televised 9-dart finish on 13 October 1984 at the MFI World Matchplay. He won £102,000 — a record prize at the time.

question: How many World Championship titles did Phil Taylor win during his career?
explanation: Phil Taylor won 16 World Championship titles — an all-time record in darts that is unlikely to ever be broken.

question: What is the maximum checkout possible in a game of darts?
explanation: The maximum checkout is 170 — Triple 20, Triple 20, Bullseye. It is the holy grail of finishing combinations in darts.

question: Which country does Michael van Gerwen represent?
explanation: Michael van Gerwen represents the Netherlands. He was born in Vlijmen and is widely regarded as the greatest player of the modern era.

question: What is the official throwing distance from the oche to the dartboard?
explanation: The official throwing distance is 2.37 metres from the front of the board to the oche — standardised across all professional tournaments worldwide.

question: What score does the bullseye carry in darts?
explanation: The bullseye scores 50 points. The outer bull scores 25. Most professionals aim for Triple 20 as their primary scoring target rather than the bull.

question: Who was the first woman to beat a man at the PDC World Championship?
explanation: Fallon Sherrock made history at the 2019 PDC World Championship by becoming the first woman ever to beat a man at the tournament — defeating Ted Evetts in round one.

explanation: Peter Wright is nicknamed Snakebite — a nod to his colourful, snake-themed outfits and hairstyles that make him one of the most recognisable players in the sport.

explanation: Luke Littler won the PDC World Championship in January 2025 at the age of 17 — making him the youngest world champion in the tournament's history.

question: What is the highest number of points you can score with a single dart?
explanation: The maximum score with a single dart is 60 — the Triple 20. This is why professional players always aim for the top of the board as their primary scoring target.

question: Which venue hosts the PDC World Darts Championship every year?
explanation: The PDC World Championship is held every year at Alexandra Palace in North London — known affectionately as Ally Pally. It is one of the most iconic venues in world sport.

question: How many points must a player start from in a standard professional darts game?
explanation: A standard professional darts game starts at 501 points. Players must reach exactly zero, finishing on a double or the bullseye.

question: Which player is nicknamed The Flying Scotsman?
explanation: Gary Anderson is nicknamed The Flying Scotsman. The two-time World Champion from Forfar is one of the most naturally talented players ever to pick up a dart.

question: How many sections are there on a standard dartboard?
explanation: There are 20 numbered sections on a standard dartboard, ranging from 1 to 20. The layout is designed so high numbers are surrounded by low numbers to penalise inaccuracy.

question: What profession did Gerwyn Price have before becoming a professional darts player?
explanation: Gerwyn Price played professional rugby union in Wales before switching to darts. He only turned professional at darts in his late 20s — making his World Championship title even more remarkable.

question: What year was the PDC — Professional Darts Corporation — founded?
explanation: The PDC was founded in 1993 when 16 of the world's top players broke away from the BDO to form the World Darts Council — later renamed the Professional Darts Corporation.

question: Which player is nicknamed Barney?
explanation: Raymond van Barneveld is known as Barney. The Dutch legend won 5 World Championship titles and is best remembered for his iconic 7-6 victory over Phil Taylor in the 2007 PDC World final.

question: What height must the centre of a dartboard be mounted from the floor?
explanation: The centre of a dartboard — the bullseye — must be mounted exactly 1.73 metres from the floor in all professional tournaments. This is standardised worldwide.

question: What is the outer bull worth in a game of darts?
explanation: The outer bull — also called the 25 ring or bull — is worth 25 points. The inner bullseye is worth 50 points and counts as a double for checkout purposes.

question: What is the minimum number of darts needed to finish a game of 501?
explanation: The minimum number of darts needed to finish a game of 501 is 9 — known as a nine-dart finish or perfect leg. It is the holy grail of darts and one of sport's rarest achievements.

question: What sport did Jonny Clayton work in before becoming a professional darts player?
explanation: Jonny Clayton — The Ferret — worked as a plasterer before turning professional at darts. He went on to win the Premier League and Grand Slam of Darts in the same year he went full time.
